title: Executive Recruiter

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can generate long-lists and map talent markets, but courting confidential C-suite candidates, closing negotiations, and board-level trust-building resist automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: General AI market-mapping and sourcing tools apply, but no dedicated executive-search product has real traction beyond assisting research.
rationales.adoption: Executive search remains a relationship-first, low-adoption niche; most firms use AI only for background research, not candidate engagement.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can build longlists and compile market-intelligence briefs, but the confidential, high-trust engagement work stays entirely human.

tasksAtRisk: Market-mapping and long-list generation from AI-searched public data
Background research and compensation benchmarking on shortlisted candidates
Search-status reporting and pipeline documentation for the client
tasksGrowing: Confidential C-suite candidate engagement and trust-building
Board-level search strategy and succession-planning advisory
Offer negotiation and counter-offer management for senior hires
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1071 (Human Resources Specialists), p75 extended toward executive-search market postings
insight: AI compiles the market maps and background research that used to take a search associate weeks, but persuading a sitting CEO to consider a move is not a prompt-able task. Executive recruiters who lean into board relationships and succession advisory are among the least exposed roles in recruiting.
